tickerText: [TENNIS] HSBC Championships championship Sunday at The Queen's Club: Donna Vekić caps a lucky-loser run with a 6-0, 7-6(8-6) win over Emma Raducanu for the WTA 500 women's title; Francisco Cerundolo and 2024 champion Tommy Paul meet in the men's final after Cerundolo rallied past Brandon Nakashima 6-7(5), 6-3, 6-4 and Paul extended his Queen's unbeaten streak to nine with a 6-3, 6-3 semi-final win over Ugo Humbert • [SQUASH] PSA Tour Finals season finale at Centquatre-Paris: Diego Elias becomes the first South American champion, beating Paul Coll 11-7, 11-4, 11-6, 11-9 after Coll took the opener; world No. 1 Hania El Hammamy claims her 22nd PSA title and seventh of 2025/26 with an 11-6, 10-11, 11-8, 11-8 win over Sivasangari Subramaniam • [BADMINTON] Macau Open finals day at the East Asian Games Dome: Kantaphon Wangcharoen faces Hu Zhe An in men's singles after his first Super 300 final in nine years; Kim Ga Eun meets Park Ga Eun in an all-Korea women's final; Indonesia's Rayhan/Wahyudi take on Jin Yong/Lee Jong Min in men's doubles to close the $370,000 BWF World Tour stop • [PICKLEBALL] Capital Securities Beijing Open Championship Sunday at China's National Tennis Center — PPA Tour Asia's Olympic-venue debut crowns five golds as top seeds Chao Yi Wang and Sahra Dennehy chase triple crowns in women's singles semis while Zane Ford, Harrison Brown, Hong Kit Wong, and Hien Truong contest the men's last four • [TABLE TENNIS] WTT Star Contender Ljubljana closes at Hala Tivoli: Japan's Shunsuke Togami and Hiroto Shinozuka edge China's Wen Ruibo and Huang Youzheng 3-2 in the men's doubles final; Zhang Benmei and Hina Hayata take women's doubles gold as Team China finishes 0-for-5 in titles with one runner-up finish

newsItems.title: Vekić and Paul Set for Queen's Championship Sunday
newsItems.excerpt: The HSBC Championships reach their climax at The Queen's Club: Donna Vekić completed a remarkable lucky-loser run to beat Emma Raducanu 6-0, 7-6(8-6) for the WTA 500 women's crown. On the men's side, Francisco Cerundolo rallied from a set down to defeat Brandon Nakashima 6-7(5), 6-3, 6-4 for his first ATP 500 final, while 2024 champion Tommy Paul stretched his Queen's winning streak to nine with a 6-3, 6-3 semi-final win over Ugo Humbert. Cerundolo and Paul meet Sunday for the ATP 500 title.

newsItems.title: Elias and El Hammamy Crown PSA Tour Finals
newsItems.excerpt: The 2025/26 PSA Squash Tour season ended in Paris with two new champions at Centquatre-Paris. Peru's Diego Elias became the first South American to win the season finale, beating New Zealand's Paul Coll 11-7, 11-4, 11-6, 11-9 after Coll took the opening game. World No. 1 Hania El Hammamy claimed her 22nd PSA title — and seventh this season — with an 11-6, 10-11, 11-8, 11-8 victory over Malaysia's Sivasangari Subramaniam, sealing her place atop the year-end rankings.

newsItems.title: Wangcharoen Ends Drought as Macau Open Finals Begin
newsItems.excerpt: Kantaphon Wangcharoen reached his first BWF World Tour final in more than nine years at the Macau Open, beating Indonesia's Muhamad Yusuf 22-20, 21-15 to set up a men's singles title match against Hu Zhe An. Sunday's $370,000 finals at the East Asian Games Dome also feature an all-Korea women's singles clash between Kim Ga Eun and Park Ga Eun, plus a men's doubles decider between Indonesia's Ali Faathir Rayhan and Devin Artha Wahyudi and Korea's Jin Yong and Lee Jong Min.

newsItems.title: Beijing Open Crowns Champions at Olympic Venue
newsItems.excerpt: Championship Sunday at the Capital Securities Beijing Open brings PPA Tour Asia's first visit to China's National Tennis Center to a close with medal matches across all pro divisions. Top seeds Chao Yi Wang and Sahra Dennehy remain in contention for triple crowns after advancing to women's singles semi-finals, while Zane Ford, Harrison Brown, Hong Kit Wong, and Hien Truong contest the men's last four at the Olympic Green venue that hosted the 2008 Games.
